adversarial_bypass_checks: non-inspectable payload fields through generic transaction routes reject with OPAQUE_PROTOCOL_PAYLOAD_UNSUPPORTED
restricted-read group state through legacy fields rejects with NON_PUBLIC_GROUP_UNSUPPORTED or PUBLIC_READ_VISIBILITY_REQUIRED
metadata-embedded opaque payload fields reject recursively
attachment references that include sealed or opaque payload fields reject recursively
frontend route removal is backed by backend replay enforcement
removed legacy communication tx names are absent from canon and reject as unknown
legacy restricted account-feed and scoped-content archives are not readable through owner-authenticated routes
media and dispute evidence CID fields must be valid public content-addressed references, never opaque URLs or local-only handles
permission probes use public-content share gates instead of removed communication payloads
helper contract artifacts expose no removed communication state subjects

expected_enforcement_literals: tests that assert non-inspectable or restricted-read payload rejection
frontend guards that fail if removed communication modules return
non_social_identity_evidence_terms: reviewer_restricted_evidence remains a restricted identity evidence compatibility field, not a protocol-native social or non-public group surface

public_only_rule: All protocol-native social, civic, governance, moderation, dispute, group, reputation, and validator/operator activity must be publicly inspectable. Membership may gate participation but never read visibility.

remaining_non_social_boundaries.reason: Raw identity-verification evidence is session scoped while public consensus surfaces expose commitments, receipts, status, and review outcomes. It must not create non-public groups, user-to-user hidden notes, or hidden social/governance/reputation meaning.
Observer tx queue rows are durable tx forwarding records and are not user-to-user communication threads.
Legacy helper receipt verification signs helper receipts and does not hide protocol-native social content.

retired_communication_surfaces_removed: Legacy person-to-person protocol communication tx names are no longer canonical.
Legacy thread read routes are unmounted; public notices use /v1/activity/notices.
Frontend communication pages, cryptographic communication helpers, and key bootstrappers are removed.
Group reads are forced public; restricted-read settings are rejected.
Account feed and scoped content compatibility reads do not expose owner-restricted or membership-restricted archives.
